preview_text:
The model favors England at 63%, with Panama holding an 12% chance of victory. The underdog's path to three points is materially constrained, though not negligible in a single-match setting.

The 24.8% draw probability suggests the match carries meaningful competitive balance in its middle outcome. England's win probability exceeds the draw chance by roughly 38 percentage points, indicating the model expects England as the clearer favorite but does not rule out stalemate as a plausible result. The 11–12% range for a Panama upset reflects genuine uncertainty inherent in knockout football.

The sportsbook consensus diverges from the model, assigning England 73.6% and Panama 9.9%—a gap of roughly 10 percentage points favoring the favorite and compressing the draw to 16.5%. Polymarket prices align more closely with sportsbooks than with the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects true match probability; the divergence warrants noting for users comparing forecasts.
